sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Espanyol are struggling, having failed to win in their last 4 matches, with 3 losses and 1 draw, conceding in all 5 recent games. Deportivo Alavés show mixed form, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses in their last 5, but they’ve managed to score consistently. This suggests a slight edge for Alavés in terms of momentum.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, each team has secured a win, with Alavés triumphing 2-1 in their most recent clash in November 2025. Historically, matches between these sides are competitive, with no clean sheets for Alavés in the last 6 meetings, indicating goals are likely on both ends.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Espanyol, sitting 5th with 34 points, boast a balanced attack and defense (25 goals scored and conceded), ranking well in headers and interceptions. Alavés, 15th with 22 points, struggle offensively with only 18 goals scored, but their recent form suggests improvement. Espanyol’s home advantage at RCDE Stadium could be key.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Espanyol’s Pere Milla (6 goals) and Edu Expósito (5 assists) are critical to their attack, while Alavés rely on Lucas Boyé (4 goals, standout in H2H with a 7.2 rating). Their contributions could decide the outcome of this tight contest.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Espanyol’s strength in penalty area entries and accurate crosses (2nd in LaLiga) could exploit Alavés’ defensive vulnerabilities. Alavés, with decent final third pressing, may look to counterattack, but their lower conversion rate might limit their threat.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (43% home win, 31% draw, 26% away win) and Espanyol’s superior league standing, they are favorites at home. However, Alavés’ recent resilience and H2H competitiveness suggest a close match, likely with under 2.5 goals based on recent trends for both teams.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half summary
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Espanyol and Deportivo Alavés at the RCDE Stadium ended in a 1-1 draw, reflecting a balanced contest. Espanyol struck first with a headed goal from Roberto Fernández early in the game, setting the tone for their attacking intent. However, Deportivo Alavés responded through Antonio Blanco’s well-taken left-footed strike, leveling the score before the break. Both teams showcased moments of promise, but neither could capitalize further despite their efforts in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés held a slight edge in possession and demonstrated a stronger presence in the final third with more shots on target. Their ability to win a higher percentage of duels, especially in the air, suggests a more physical approach to the game. Espanyol, on the other hand, showed intent with frequent entries into the penalty area and a decent number of final third entries, indicating they were not far behind in creating opportunities. The hosts also managed to keep a clean sheet in terms of disciplinary actions, while Alavés picked up a yellow card, hinting at a slightly more aggressive style.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Espanyol, Roberto Fernández stood out as the main attacking threat, having already found the back of the net with a clinical header. His impact in the first half makes him a focal point for the second. On the Alavés side, Antonio Blanco was exceptional, not only scoring the equalizer but also contributing defensively with interceptions and tackles. Additionally, Toni Martínez showed sharpness with multiple efforts on goal, posing a constant danger to the Espanyol defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Alavés pushing forward aggressively, dominating the early minutes with sustained attacking spells. Espanyol responded with a shift in momentum around the time of their goal, enjoying a period of control as they pressed for openings. The momentum swung back to Alavés as they built pressure leading up to their equalizer, showcasing a strong attacking phase. Towards the end of the half, Espanyol regained some initiative with a series of forward moves, while Alavés had brief moments of threat before the whistle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas of improvement
halftimeSections.text: Espanyol will need to convert their penalty area entries into clearer chances if they hope to take the lead in the second half. Their duel-winning percentage also needs improvement to counter Alavés’ physicality. For Alavés, while they’ve been more clinical in attack, they must maintain consistency in their defensive structure to prevent Espanyol from capitalizing on transitional plays. Turning their higher possession into more big chances will be crucial for them to edge ahead.
